Hi,

| have just recently began to research my family heritage and came across your name on burial sites of what
would be my Grandaa's (Onnie Nunley also spelled Nunneley) father and brother. | was wondering if you had
any information you could share on the Nunneley's. His dad was Joseph Alexander Nunneley Jr and
Clementine Nunneley. My Mother was Ruth Nunley daughter of Onnie and Grace Grigg Nunley.

My Mom passed away 2 years ago so | don't have access to much information on my family. We have always
spelled their last name as Nunley but see where it has also been spelled Nunneley. On a 1930 census my
Grandad was also listed as Clemmie Nunniley.

! would appreciate any information you could share with me. Thank you for your time.

Jan Barker

Subject: Mary Nunnelly 1846-1918 Ark—-Texas\
From: Joe Carroll (ery1555@gmail.com)\
To: nunne002@sbcglobal.net;\
\
Date: Thursday, March 6, 2014 10:02 PM\
\
Hi Mary,\
\
Help....Help\
\
Been searching for years for the parents of my Great Grand Mother Mary Nunnelly. I saw your listing\
on FAG and looking for any info that you might have. Lots of people put my Mary as the daughter of\
Joseph Alexander Nunnelly and Nancy Easley. Sure fits easy but I have never completly bought in to\
that. Have her birth in Franklin Co Ark and living on Washburn Scott Co Ark 14 years old and with\
husband B F Bilton and child. and later in Vineyard Washington Co Ark and then in Johnson Co\
Texas. Also have seen a few on web sites that list J A Nunneley with 20 childen and always wondered\
about that.\
\
Any help or even a guess would be very much appreciative.\
Thanks\
\
Joe Carroll ery1555@gmail.com\

Name: Nancy Easley\
\
Surname: Easley\
\
Given Name: Nancy\
\
pex: E\
\
Birth: 23 Aug 1805 in Greenville Co, South Carolina\
Death: Apr 1850 in Boston, Franklin Co, Arkansas\
_UID: BDA212AA3570D511BDEF00E0293392 103065\
Note:\
\
Went to Arkansas.\
\
Posted on GenForum by Larry Bates on September 07, 1998 at 20:12:46:\
\
I am looking for information on the parents of Nancy Easly, she was born circa 1806 in\
S.C. and died in Apr 1850 of a fever.\
\
She married Joseph A Nunley,most likely in Hickman county Tenn.\
\
She may have been part Indian.\
\
She and Joseph moved to Arkansas by the mid 1830's and had lived in Carroll CO\
1838,Conway co 1840,Franklin co 1850,also in Madison and Washington counties.\
\
I am afraid thats about all I have,but I do have a picture of a picture of Joseph A\
Nunley,taken most likely around the 1860's that I have scanned and put here for you,\
thought you might get a kick out of it but its not real clear,it is in jpeg form. Larry Bates\
\
Joseph Alexander Nunley,born Sep 9 1802,in Lincoln Co N.C. and died circa 1870 in\
Arkansas.\
\
Joseph was the son of Anderson Nunley and Martha Baldwin.\
\
Nancy Easley,born Aug 23 1805,in Greenville Co S.C.,died Apr 1850 "of a fever" in\
Franklin Co Ark.\
\
Joseph was a farmer all his life,although he once served as a Sheriff in Carroll Co\
Arkansas.\
\
He was one of the pioneer residents of Carroll and Madison Counties in Arkansas.\
[from the Centennial history of Arkansas,vol 2 Clarke publishing co 1922]\
\
The 1838 tax lists for Arkansas shows him in Carroll Co.\
\
The 1840 census index shows him in Osage township,Conway Co.\
\
The 1850 census for Franklin Co,Boston township-\
\
Joseph Nunley age 47 Farmer $1,000 realestate born in N.C.\
\
Ecloe 18 born T.N. attends school\
\
Sarah 16 born T.N. attends school\

Five children already left home.\
\
Joseph's second wife was Claressa Maxwell married on May 20 1851,in Franklin Co\
Arkansas.\
\
Sam Houston Nunley,joined the Confederate army in 1861,he became 1st lieutant,was\
captured and incarcerated at camp Morton prison,in Indiana,he escaped in Aug 1864.\
\
Elizabeth "Mary" Nunley,born May 26 1846,died Jan 4 1918,married Benjamin Franklin\
Bilton,born Sep 27 1837 in Washington Co Ark,died Dec 28 1928,married on May\
31863.\
\
Mary Elizabeth Nunley is buried at the Nichols cemetary in Johnson County Texas,Mary\
and Benjamin came to Texas from Benton Co Arkansas in 1886 and settled in Johnson\
\
County Texas.\
\
Family history relates Mary Elizabeth Nunley,went by the nickname "Polly",she had black\
hair and eyes and claimed to have been part Indian.\
\

Subj: Re: Parents and siblings of Eliza Jane Nunneley Wolf

From: Nunnedd2

Though | am not actively working on the Nunneley family at this time, thought you might like to have a little info on the family,
as follows:

Joseph Alexander Nunneley (son of Anderson and Martha Baldwin Nunneley)
bom: 9 Sep 1802 / NC
died: after 1866
married: #1 - Nancy Easley (dau of Thomas and Ecloe/Ecleo Wade Easley)
bom: 23 Aug 1805/06
died: April 1850 / Franklin Co. Arkansas
Issue of Joseph and Nancy:
Adaline Nunneley - never married
bom: 7 Jul 1823 / Hickman Co. TN - 1824 / Hickman Co. TN
David Nunneley - never married
bom: 16 Aug 1824 / Hickman Co. TN - 1843 / Carroll Co. AR
Martha Nunneley married Benjamin C. Hawkins
born: 10 Dec 1825 / Hickman Co. TN - died unknown
William Nunneley - never married
bom: 10 Jan 1827 / Hickman Co. TN - 1827 / Hickman Co. TN
Thomas Nunneley / never married
bom: 11 Jul 1828 / Hickman Co. TN - 1830 / Hickman Co. TN
Elizabeth Nunneley married William Hawkins
bor: 21 Mar 1830 / Hickman Co. TN - 7 Jan 1908 / Franklin Co. AR
John Robert Foster Nunneley married Rhoda J. Guest
bom: 12 Apr 1831 / Hickman Co. TN - 12 Sep 1897 / Butte Co. CA
Ecole/Ecleo Nunneley married Simon D. Whitley
bor: 23 Aug 1832 / Hickman Co. TN - after 1870 / Milam Co. TX
Sarah Nunneley married Richard G. Steele
born: 6 Nov 1833 / Hickman Co. TN - died AR
Mahulda Nunneley married William Hamilton + 3 others
bom: 13 May 1834 / Hickman Co. TN - 9 Apr 1907 / Klickitat Co. WA
James D. Nunneley married Lucy Sparks
bom: 4 Mar 1837 / Carroll Co. AR - after 1873 in probably Wood Co. TX
NOTE: James D. was an early sheriff in Milam Co. TX
Tennessee Nunneley / never married
bom: 1 Apr 1838 / Carroll Co AR - 1840 / Carroll or Franklin Co. AR
Jackson Baldwin Nunneley married Florence Webster in Butler Co. OH
bom: 8 Oct 1839 / Carroll Co. AR - 11 Mar 1907 / Bell Co. TX
Harrison Nunneley / never married
bom: 1 Apr 1841 / Carroll or Franklin AR - 1847 / Carroll or Franklin Co. AR
Sam Houston Nunneley married Rachel Elizabeth Bowman
bom: 16 Nov 1842 / Carroll or Franklin AR - 1872 / Arkansas
ELIZA JANE NUNNELEY married Adam/John Wolf
bom: 30 Sep 1844 / Carroll or Franklin AR - died unknown
Mary "Polly" Nunneley married unknown
bom: 26 May 1846 / Carroll or Franklin AR - died unknown
George Nunneley / never married
bom: 28 Mar 1848 / Carroll or Franklin AR - died as an infant
Nancy Nunneley / never married
bom: 8 Oct 1849 / Carroll or Franklin Co. AR - 1851 / Franklin Co. AR
Joseph Alexander Nunneley (son of Anderson and Martha Baldwin Nunneley)
married # 2 - Clarissa Moore Maxwell (widow of Joel Maxwell) and daughter of
George and Mary Chapin Moore)

From: Janice Barker (janbarker1@yahoo.com)
To: nunne002@sbcglobal.net;

Date: Saturday, March 8, 2014 6:05 AM

Hi Mary,

It has been a while since I have written and writing to ask a favor of you. 2013 was a very difficult
year for my family so family history was definitely put on hold. I haven't done anything at all to be
honest. I keep my grandson who is almost 3 all week and that consumes much of my time, weekends |
rest to keep up with him. At 62 he keeps me very busy and entertained but sometimes just plain worn
out.

My son was diagnosed with a rare genetic disease last year, pompe disease and today we took our
daughter to start the testing procedure and pretty sure she will have it to but praying not. It is the late
adult onset type and in order for our children to have it my husband and I both have to have a bad
gene (dr. description). He was diagnosed at Mayo in Minnesota as 6 doctors in Kansas City could not
find out what was wrong. It has taken a major toll on Todd and really thought we would lose him if
they didn't find the problem. We finally loaded him up and took him to the emergency room at Mayo
where he spent 2 days in intensive care and a week there before the diagnosis. We were asked if we
were related and of course said no but then wondered if somewhere in our history there might be a
family tie to a Nunley and Barker marriage. I wonder if you have any record of that. Pompe manifests
itself in your muscles and breathing. Todd is breathing at 42% right

now and has to use a bipap anytime he lays down to nap or sleep. Pompe is a very hard disease to
diagnois and usually takes years to get a diagnois and the dr. said years ago was unheard of or so rare
people would contribute it to maybe a heart attack. I hope you don't mind me asking. I know if
somewhere I inherited a defective gene then there are others who carry it also and the only way it will
manifest itself if they marry someone else with a defective gene and their child will have a 25%
chance of having pompe disease. Genetics to me is complicated but believe our grandson will at least
be a carrier of this disease and will need genetic testing done when he gets married. On top of all this
a week after his diagnosis I fell and broke my left hip and had total hip replacement then daughter had
to have a thyroid gland removed. The dr. was very confident based on testing it was cancer but God
gave us a break and it wasn't so only had to have one

gland removed. Now my mother in law has leukemia and given a few months to live and is currently
in the hospital undergoing chemo. Life can sure be full of challenges and even if somewhere back in
our past Nunley/Barker did marry it isn't going to change anything but might explain how we are both
carriers.

Sorry this is so long but couldn't sleep (as usual) and thought I would ask for your help in this. I hope
you are doing well and my brother is in the process of moving to Neosho so maybe we will make that
trip we talked about and visit with you. Thank you in advance for your help and look forward to
hearing from you. Hoping if you pray you will put my children in your prayers. There isn't a cure for
this just enzyme replacement therapy but doesn't seem to be helping Todd. He takes infusions every
other week and will for life, the cost for his medicine (just medicine) right now is $18,000 per
infusion and it is based on weight. I doubt he weight 120 pounds right now. He is going to Duke
University in April if his health holds out enough to see the leading genetics in pompe disease and
hoping for some suggestions to improve his health. I can't tell you the guilt as a parent that goes along
with this to know I passed this on to him and probably Kyndra

too, unknowingly but never the less I carry it.

On Fri, 3/1/13, Mary R. M. Nunneley <nunne002@sbcglobal.net> wrote:

Subject: Re: Nunneley/Nunley
To: "Janice Barker" <janbarker!@yahoo.com>
Date: Friday, March 1, 2013, 5:49 PM

Would love to have a visit from

you and your brother. Just let me know when you might
come to Bartlesville.

No, we did not know Onnie and Grace. Sure my
husband's grandparents, E. A. and Sennie probably knew
them. Grandpa and Grandma moved from Arkansas about
1898 to Oklahoma, but still had much contact with the
Arkansas family.

The name Nunneley has been found with 14 various
spellings over the years since they lived in Virginia in the
late 1600s. I remember Grandpa Nunneley being very
upset that his brother spelled his name Nunley. When I
got into genealogy research, I was so amused when I| found
Elbert Alexander Nunneley who married Melcenia Jane
McCollough in Arkansas. Yep....Grandpa and Grandma
married as "E.A. Nunley and Senie

McCollough". Glad I found it after Grandpa died,

so then I did not have to show it to him.

My grandbabes keep telling me I am a very young
81....not so. I had a heart attack last year and it

sure did take the starch out of this lady. But, still
keeping busy.

Talk later,

Mary R M Nunneley

Good Day Mary,

I am amazed you are 81 and using internet. My Mom

would never touch a computer. I am 61 and afraid I am not
keeping up with all the technology changes. I appreciate
your offer to share information. I told my brother

Wayne about your information and suggested sometime we go
visit you and hear about our relatives. He lives at
Warrensburg and his wife has offered to help me work on our
family tree. I know with the world we live in one

cannot be too careful about strangers and totally understand
if you don't want to. It would be spring before we would
attempt to make that trip. Him and his wife had the flu
followed up by a virus so they still aren't feeling

well.

My oldest brother James is 72 and remembers the family
gatherings better than I do. We used to go to our
Grandparents every Sunday and have dinner, all the family
except Norma (Virginia) who lived in Kansas. I meant to tell
you I believe her name is Virginia but goes by Norma.

Do you know if you ever met Onnie and Grace? The
Nunley name is slowly dwindling on Onnie's

side. My Uncle Bill (Bobby Gene) had a son Robert and

he had a son Robert Jr. who was killed in a car wreck when
he was I believe 16. He also is buried at New Bethel. I

think he may have another son but not sure but if he

doesn't that ends the Nunley name from Onnie.

Hope I won't bore you to death. I used

to work in banking then quit to keep my grandson and we
don't get out much so really miss interaction with

people. I was curious if you knew how Nunneley became
Nunley.

Have

a great day.

Jan

Barker

Subj: Re: MY, MY MAHULDA\
\
Date: 2/14/04 10:57:04 AM Central Standard Time\
From: Nunne00d2\
\
To: MILWILEST\
\
Just received your interesting packet of material. | have not had time to read it all, but it is the most interesting Nunneley\
material | have ever read, so far. Mahulda was certainly a character +. One of the things | did note was her first divorce\
mentioned that her husband went to CA........ this proves what | have always thought. Several of the Nunneley men and in-\
laws went to CA in the late 1850s.\
\
The picture of Joseph Alexander Nunneley ??? - could be, but how do we prove it ?\
\
A man by the name of Larry Bates is supposed to have a picture of Joseph. If you could find him and compare the pictures, it\
might be valuable. Larry has stated that Elizabeth Mary or Mary Elizabeth Nunley, dau of Joseph and Nancy Easley Nunley\
married Benjamin Franklin Bilton. Much of this information was posted on Ancestry by Will Johnson (a professional\
genealogist). | have had contact with Mr. Johnson with a request for him to make corrections to material concerning my\
family that he has posted with lots of errors. He refuses.......... but did want me to copy everything | had. Not very\
professional, me thinks.\
\
The Elizabeth Mary/Mary Elizabeth Nunley who married B. F. Bilton is listed on the 1900 census for Johnson Co. TX Her\
birthdate is listed as Nov 1844, born in Ark. and her father born in Kentucky and her mother bom in Arkansas. On the 1880\
census, she says that both of her parents were born in Georgia. The one thing that bothers me about this is that she is listed\
as MARY C. on the census and we also, know that Mary (dau of Jos. and Nancy) was born on 26 May 1846, not Nov. 1844.\
Mary's sister, Eliza was born in 1844.\
\
Speaking of Eliza, finally found her and family in Illinois in 1880. From the births of the children, they apparently moved from\
Butler Co. OH to Lawrence Co. IL about 1872/3. Have not looked for them in 1900.\
\
Thanks for the goodies and will spend some time "reading"........\
\
Mary R M Nunneley\
\
SES\

Dear Jo,\
\
Finally home from my summer travels and back to genealogy. After\
returning from Texas, I spent some time with our son and his family in\
southwestern Oklahoma. I returned last Monday and am trying to catch\
up on needed correspondence before our daughter has her second baby\
Nov 1. Our little Sarah is only two, so know Kathleen will need some\
help with two little ones. iT APPEARS\
\
I have found a little on James\ Nunneley, but very little. From the\
census record, 1880 Titus Co. TX/“that Lucy Sparks Nunneley, widow of\
James, married #2 a Robert H. Cochran. You said on your family group\
sheet for James that she married #2 John Cochran. Also, according to\
the census, Ida is shown as being older than Joseph.. It appears that\
she was born ca 1871 and the 1900 census says that Joseph was born in\
Dec 1873. I also noted on the 1900 census that Joseph stated that he\
was the father of four children, only three living. Wonder what the\
name of the fourth child could be??\
\
I was surprised to find Joseph in Kiowa Co., OK in 1910. I had\
ordered the 1910 Soundex to look for my husband's Nunneleys and was\
delighted to find Joseph A. When I went to see my son and family, I\
went through Kiowa Co. and over a small bridge and stream...OTTER\
CREEK. The same name as the 1910 township where Joseph and Lilly\
lived.\
\
Dick's great-grandfather, Lawson Harrison Nunneley, lived in Washita\
Co. which adjoins Kiowa Co. He apparently moved from Oklahoma\
Territory to New Mexico and about 1910 (after the census) moved to\
Mayfield, Beckham Co., OK. He later moved to Foss, Washita Co., but I\
am not sure as yet exactly what year. James and Lawson were\
half-brothers so may have had contact when living in the same area of\
Okiahoma. Several distant cousins(on both sides of Dick's family)\
still live in that area. I stopped on my way back to Bartlesville and\
visited with a his cousin. It was our first meeting, though she has\
provided good genealogical help by letter for several years.\
\
Will close for now and please let me know about Lucy's\
husband...Robert? oc John? Also, do you know anything about Joseph's\
sister, Ida? Who did she marry??\
\
Will keep looking.\
\
Sincerely,\
\
Ah ane\
Mary Nunneléy\

\
Dear Jo,\
\
Sending the first material concerning Joseph Nunneley. I decided to\
copy what I have on him and will send the material I have on some of\
the children as soon as I get it copied.\
\
Your information on James being in Bell Co., Texas, arrived at just\
the right time. My vacation was to spend a week with my aunt in\
Temple, TX. I was so surorised to find it in Bell Co. I did not\
find anything on James, but are you ready...Jackson Baldwin Nunneley\
was a founder of Temple, Texas. I found a great deal of information\
on him and even found his grave in Temple. Every book I picked up at\
the library in Temple seemed to mention him. Aiso, the railroad\
museum had several items listing Jackson as a founder of Temple.\
Will send all of that material when I do the children's records.\
\
On the way home, I drove about 100 miles out of the way to go to\
Quitman (the county seat of Wood Co.). ‘Their records, marciages,\
deeds, etc., all seem to begin about i187. The cleck at tie\
courthouse thought there had been a fire at one time. f found no\
mention of James or Isaac Sparks. The courthouse hac a four volume\
set of Wood County cemetery records...they were not listed in those\
\
volumes.\
\
Will close foe now and hope you did not give up on me sending the\
\
=\
\
matecial. It will take some time to copy the children's material,\
but will as soon as possible. I do not have a copy machine, so must\
go to the library. I have my own microfilm reader, so can go that\
None -\
\
Anxious to hear fro you after you vead though tne material.\
\
Sincerely,\
\
Mary Nunneley\

THE TWENTY-THIRD PSALM\
The Lord is my shepherd; | shall not want.\
\
He maketh me to lie down in green pastures: He leadeth me\
beside the still waters.\
\
He restoreth my soul; He leadeth me in the paths of righteous-\
ness for his name’s sake\
\
Yea; tho | walk through the valley of the shadow of death, |\
will fear no evil: for thou art with me; Thy rod and Thy\
staff, they comfort me.\
\
Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine\
enemies: Thou anointest my head with oil; my cup run-\
neth over.\
\
Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my\
life: and | will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.\
